description: The debate centered on whether the Linux kernel and open-source desktop environments (GNOME/KDE) could realistically replace proprietary Microsoft Windows for general enterprise usage. Following the 2002 memo leaks and the 2004 Ubuntu launch, momentum shifted toward open source, though proprietary lock-in remained dominant in corporate sectors.

key_events.description: Microsoft 'Get the Facts' campaign launched to counter Linux adoption.
Release of Ubuntu 4.10, which simplified Linux for non-technical users.
Major corporate enterprise contracts reaffirm Windows dominance.

calibration_notes: Useful for assessing the 'tipping point' fallacy in technology adoption and proprietary lock-in.
